deductions.description: Cast angle slightly below horizontal (approx 170-175 degrees).
Slight pike in hips during initial swing for dismount.
Knees apart ('cowboy knees') in tuck position.
Small hop forward on landing.
Chest slightly down on landing.

deduction_log.reason: Low cast, form breaks in tuck, and landing control issues.

special_requirements.comment: Low Bar Kip and Long Hang Kip performed.
Back Hip Circle and Front Hip Circle performed.
Jump from Low Bar to High Bar performed.

artistry.notes: Overall flow and rhythm were good, but consistent minor form breaks (bent knees, flexed feet, low casts) detracted from the quality of movement and body tension, leading to a minor artistry deduction.

coaching_summary: This Xcel Gold bars routine demonstrates a good sequence of skills and meets all special requirements. The athlete shows consistent effort and a decent rhythm throughout the routine. However, there are pervasive form breaks, particularly in body tension (bent knees, flexed feet, slight pikes) and cast amplitude, which accumulate significant deductions. The dismount also shows form issues and a landing adjustment. Addressing these fundamental body positions will significantly elevate the execution score and prepare the athlete for higher levels.
top_3_fixes: **Improve Body Tension and Extension:** Focus on actively locking knees and pointing toes throughout all swings, casts, and flight elements. This is the most frequent micro-deduction.
**Increase Cast Amplitude to Horizontal:** Work on achieving a true horizontal cast (180 degrees) from both bars. This requires stronger core engagement and a more aggressive push-away from the bar.
**Refine Dismount Form and Landing:** Concentrate on maintaining a tight tuck with knees together during the flyaway and practicing a 'stuck' landing with a stable chest-up position.
celebrations: Successfully completed all required skills and connections without a fall.
Demonstrated good rhythm and flow between skills, avoiding major pauses.
Executed two different circling elements (Front and Back Hip Circles).
